# 👁️ Multimodal (195)

Where models stop reading and start seeing, hearing, and reasoning across pixels, text, and video at once. This is the frontier where AI perceives the world the way you do, and the repos here let you build it.

[↑ Back to index](../README.md)

## VLM

Vision-language models that look at an image and tell you what's really going on

<details>
<summary>Show 43 repos</summary>

- [openai/CLIP](https://github.com/openai/CLIP) — ⭐ 33,562 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Model Weights — This repository contains CLIP, a foundational vision model that learns visual concepts from natural language supervision. It's used for zero-shot image classification and understanding by predicting the most relevant text snippet given an image.
- [bytedance/UI-TARS-desktop](https://github.com/bytedance/UI-TARS-desktop) — ⭐ 29,637 · `TypeScript` · AI Agent — UI-TARS-desktop is an open-source multimodal AI agent stack that connects advanced AI models with agent infrastructure, enabling agents to interact with graphical user interfaces, perform computer operations, and use browser controls leveraging multimodal vision capabilities.
- [OpenBMB/MiniCPM-V](https://github.com/OpenBMB/MiniCPM-V) — ⭐ 24,542 · `Python` · Model Weights — A pocket-sized, open-source Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) designed for ultra-efficient image and video understanding on mobile devices, excelling in benchmarks against larger models.
- [jundot/omlx](https://github.com/jundot/omlx) — ⭐ 13,843 · `Python` · Self-hosted Service — An LLM inference server designed for Apple Silicon, featuring continuous batching, SSD caching, and a macOS menu bar interface. It enhances local AI performance with support for various models, including vision-language models, and integrates with coding tools like Copilot, Claude, and Codex.
- [OpenGVLab/InternVL](https://github.com/OpenGVLab/InternVL) — ⭐ 10,034 · `Python` · Model Weights — InternVL is an open-source family of vision-language models designed as an alternative to GPT-4o, capable of multimodal dialogue and tasks like image classification, video classification, semantic segmentation, and image-text retrieval.
- [Blaizzy/mlx-vlm](https://github.com/Blaizzy/mlx-vlm) — ⭐ 4,615 · `Python` · Library — MLX-VLM is a Python package for efficient inference and fine-tuning of Vision Language Models (VLMs) on Apple Silicon Macs using the MLX framework, supporting various VLMs with continuous batching and distributed inference.
- [openclaw/Peekaboo](https://github.com/openclaw/Peekaboo) — ⭐ 4,559 · `Swift` · MCP Server — Peekaboo is a macOS CLI and optional MCP server designed to empower AI agents by enabling them to capture screenshots of the system and applications. It facilitates visual question answering through local or remote AI models and allows agents to control the macOS interface.
- [IDEA-Research/Rex-Omni](https://github.com/IDEA-Research/Rex-Omni) — ⭐ 1,355 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — A 3-billion parameter Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) that utilizes next point prediction for open-set object detection, capable of detecting a wide range of objects.
- [NVlabs/Eagle](https://github.com/NVlabs/Eagle) — ⭐ 1,022 · `Python` · Paper Code — Eagle is a repository for frontier vision-language models, focusing on data-centric strategies for their development and implementation. It provides code and potentially models for large multimodal models.
- [jmerelnyc/Photo-agents](https://github.com/jmerelnyc/Photo-agents) — ⭐ 1,021 · `Python` · AI Agent — This repository provides code for autonomous, self-evolving LLM agents that can operate a computer, utilizing vision-grounded layered memory and the ability to write their own skills.
- [EvolvingLMMs-Lab/LLaVA-OneVision-2](https://github.com/EvolvingLMMs-Lab/LLaVA-OneVision-2) — ⭐ 835 · `Python` · Framework — LLaVA-OneVision-2 is an open framework designed for democratized multimodal training, particularly focusing on vision-language models and video understanding tasks. It aims to provide tools and implementations for developing multimodal AI.
- [EvolvingLMMs-Lab/NEO](https://github.com/EvolvingLMMs-Lab/NEO) — ⭐ 772 · `Python` · Paper Code — NEO is a series of native encoder-free multimodal models designed from first principles, capable of processing image, video, and 3D data. It aims to achieve state-of-the-art performance in spatial intelligence and compares favorably to other multimodal LLMs.
- [tiiuae/Falcon-Perception](https://github.com/tiiuae/Falcon-Perception) — ⭐ 670 · `Python` · Library — This repository provides inference code for Falcon-Perception and Falcon-OCR models, which are natively multimodal, autoregressive transformer models. They are designed to perform object detection and OCR from natural language inputs.
- [ml-explore/mlx-swift-lm](https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-swift-lm) — ⭐ 660 · `Swift` · Framework — This repository provides tools and examples for integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision Language Models (VLMs) into Swift applications, leveraging the MLX framework.
- [MMMU-Benchmark/MMMU](https://github.com/MMMU-Benchmark/MMMU) — ⭐ 588 · `Python` · Paper Code — MMMU provides evaluation code for a massive, multi-discipline multimodal understanding and reasoning benchmark for expert AGI. It is designed to assess the capabilities of large multimodal models on complex tasks spanning various academic fields.
- [linzhiqiu/t2v\_metrics](https://github.com/linzhiqiu/t2v_metrics) — ⭐ 583 · `Python` · Library — This repository provides a framework for evaluating text-to-image, text-to-video, and text-to-3D generative models using VQAScore. It serves as an evaluation metric and reward model for visual generation tasks, offering a quantitative measure of generated content quality and prompt alignment.
- [allenai/molmo2](https://github.com/allenai/molmo2) — ⭐ 568 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the code for Molmo2, a Vision-Language Model (VLM). It's associated with a research paper detailing the model's architecture and capabilities.
- [jordanrendric/claude-video-vision](https://github.com/jordanrendric/claude-video-vision) — ⭐ 485 · `TypeScript` · Plugin/Extension — This plugin allows Claude Code to analyze and understand video content by extracting video frames and performing multimodal audio analysis. It enables Claude to "watch" videos and generate insights from both visual and auditory information.
- [EvolvingLMMs-Lab/EgoLife](https://github.com/EvolvingLMMs-Lab/EgoLife) — ⭐ 435 · `Python` · Paper Code — EgoLife is a research project that proposes an egocentric life assistant, leveraging omnimodal capabilities and RAG to process first-person visual data and provide intelligent assistance. It was recognized with a distinguished paper award at CVPR.
- [zhaochen0110/OpenThinkIMG](https://github.com/zhaochen0110/OpenThinkIMG) — ⭐ 373 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Framework — An open-source framework that enables Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) to actively use adaptive vision tools, empowering them to think with and process images more effectively.
- [OpenMOSS/MOSS-VL](https://github.com/OpenMOSS/MOSS-VL) — ⭐ 279 · `Python` · Model Weights — MOSS-VL is a core multimodal model series focused on visual understanding, supporting text, single/multiple images, single/multiple videos, and interleaved visual-text inputs in Chinese and English.
- [Roots-Automation/GutenOCR](https://github.com/Roots-Automation/GutenOCR) — ⭐ 185 · `Python` · Framework — This repository provides open-source tools for training and evaluating Vision Language Models specifically designed for Optical Character Recognition (OCR).
- [ailuntx/Thinking-with-Visual-Primitives](https://github.com/ailuntx/Thinking-with-Visual-Primitives) — ⭐ 171 · `Makefile` · Paper Code — This repository contains an archived snapshot of the code accompanying the paper "Thinking with Visual Primitives," focusing on deepseek, grounding, multimodal, spatial reasoning, and vision-language models. It provides the implementation for research into multimodal AI.
- [jd-opensource/JoyAI-VL-Interaction](https://github.com/jd-opensource/JoyAI-VL-Interaction) — ⭐ 166 · Paper Code — A research project providing an 8B vision-language model, training recipes, data, and a deployable system for video interaction. It is associated with a scientific paper.
- [SeanWong17/album-assetizer](https://github.com/SeanWong17/album-assetizer) — ⭐ 152 · `Python` · CLI Tool — A Python CLI tool that scans local photo albums, uses a visual large model to generate structured semantic descriptions, and extracts metadata like EXIF and GPS, making photos easily searchable and manageable.
- [gonemedia/aipointer](https://github.com/gonemedia/aipointer) — ⭐ 127 · `TypeScript` · Application — An AI cursor assistant for macOS, Windows, and Linux that allows users to ask questions about screen content via a hotkey, using vision-capable AI models from OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini, with a focus on privacy and local-first operation.
- [ZiyuGuo99/ATLAS](https://github.com/ZiyuGuo99/ATLAS) — ⭐ 124 · Paper Code — This repository presents ATLAS, a novel method for visual reasoning that utilizes a single discrete "functional token" to perform agentic actions and latent reasoning without generating images or code. It aims to simplify visual reasoning processes by integrating the token as both an action and reasoning unit.
- [fudan-generative-vision/Bard-VL](https://github.com/fudan-generative-vision/Bard-VL) — ⭐ 86 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository introduces BARD, a method that converts existing autoregressive Vision-Language Models (VLMs) into faster, diffusion-based models. It aims to combine the intelligence of autoregressive models with the generation speed of diffusion models through progressive block merging.
- [Brandon-Collins7/sketchvlm](https://github.com/Brandon-Collins7/sketchvlm) — ⭐ 76 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — SketchVLM is a newly released model trained to generate non-destructive, editable SVG overlays on input images. Its purpose is to visually explain answers by performing tasks like part labeling, connecting-the-dots, and drawing shapes around objects.
- [bytedance/Lance](https://github.com/bytedance/Lance) — ⭐ 70 · `Python` · Library — Lance is a lightweight, native, and unified multimodal model designed for understanding, generating, and editing both images and videos.
- [OpenBMB/MiniCPM-V-Apps](https://github.com/OpenBMB/MiniCPM-V-Apps) — ⭐ 62 · `Swift` · Application — This repository likely contains applications or code for MiniCPM-V 4.6, an efficient 1.3B parameter multimodal large language model (VLM) optimized for high-resolution visual processing on consumer and mobile hardware, enabling native edge deployment.
- [yangg1224/smart\_remarkable](https://github.com/yangg1224/smart_remarkable) — ⭐ 53 · `Rust` · Application — This Rust-based vision-language agent for reMarkable e-ink tablets captures handwritten notes, sends them to a visual LLM, and displays the response on the tablet, integrating AI with a physical productivity device.
- [QwenLM/Qwen-VLA](https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen-VLA) — ⭐ 46 · Paper Code — Qwen-VLA is a Vision-Language Model (VLM) developed by QwenLM. It's designed to process and understand both visual and textual information, enabling capabilities for embodied AI tasks such as robotics and intelligent agents.
- [vlm-run/mm](https://github.com/vlm-run/mm) — ⭐ 44 · `Python` · CLI Tool — mm is a multimodal context toolkit for AI agents. It provides Unix-like command-line tools (like find, cat, grep) that are re-engineered to process various file types, including image directories, long videos, and visually-rich PDFs, making this information accessible to LLMs that cannot read them natively.
- [Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy-Embodied-RxBrain-1.0](https://github.com/Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy-Embodied-RxBrain-1.0) — ⭐ 32 · `Python` · Model Weights — This repository provides an embodied cognition foundation model called RxBrain, which features joint language-visual reasoning and imagination capabilities. It aims to bridge understanding between language and visual data for more intelligent agents.
- [ybb6/laser](https://github.com/ybb6/laser) — ⭐ 29 · `Python` · Paper Code — Laser is a new approach to visual reasoning that achieves significantly higher efficiency and performance compared to existing top models, utilizing a "forest-before-trees" reasoning method to maintain a big-picture understanding.
- [UIUC-MONET/vlm-circuit-tracing](https://github.com/UIUC-MONET/vlm-circuit-tracing) — ⭐ 22 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This repository provides code for "Circuit Tracing in Vision-Language Models," a research project presented at CVPR 2026, aimed at understanding the internal mechanisms of VLMs.
- [RUCKBReasoning/From\_Pixels\_to\_Tokens](https://github.com/RUCKBReasoning/From_Pixels_to_Tokens) — ⭐ 18 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code for a research paper titled "From Pixels to Tokens," which systematically investigates latent action supervision techniques for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.
- [guyyariv/LaMI](https://github.com/guyyariv/LaMI) — ⭐ 18 · `Python` · Paper Code — The official code implementation for LaMI, a research project presented at ACL 2026, which focuses on augmenting Large Language Models through a method of late multi-image fusion to enhance their multimodal understanding.
- [facebookresearch/VLM3](https://github.com/facebookresearch/VLM3) — ⭐ 17 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official code implementation for the research paper "VLM³: Vision Language Models Are Native 3D Learners", exploring how Vision Language Models can effectively learn from 3D data.
- [mudler/locate-anything.cpp](https://github.com/mudler/locate-anything.cpp) — ⭐ 11 · `C++` · Library — A C++ port of the Nvidia LocateAnything-3B multimodal model, optimized for local inference using the ggml library for efficient execution on various platforms.
- [DreDabe/auto-reply](https://github.com/DreDabe/auto-reply) — ⭐ 10 · `TypeScript` · Application — This is a VLM-powered desktop intelligent auto-reply system designed to manage and automate responses for chat applications like WeChat and DingTalk. It uses visual models to read chat windows, analyze sentiment, and accumulate conversation experience to provide semi-automatic or fully automatic replies with contact routing.
- [UCSC-VLAA/VLM-CapCurriculum](https://github.com/UCSC-VLAA/VLM-CapCurriculum) — ⭐ 10 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository presents a capability curriculum designed to improve the perceptual accuracy of Vision-Language Models (VLMs), specifically addressing perception errors in models like Qwen3-VL-8B before focusing on reasoning.

</details>

## World Models

Systems that learn the physics and dynamics of an environment, then dream the next frame

<details>
<summary>Show 27 repos</summary>

- [screenpipe/screenpipe](https://github.com/screenpipe/screenpipe) — ⭐ 19,004 · `Rust` · Application — Screenpipe is a local-first AI application that continuously records your screen, audio, and keyboard activity to create a searchable and summarizable memory of your digital life. It allows natural language queries about past activities and can integrate with LLMs to provide context for personal assistance.
- [NVIDIA/cosmos](https://github.com/NVIDIA/cosmos) — ⭐ 8,113 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Framework — NVIDIA Cosmos is an open platform providing omnimodal world models, datasets, and tools for building Physical AI applications. These models are designed to jointly process and generate language, image, video, audio, and action sequences, supporting development for robotics, autonomous vehicles, and smart infrastructure.
- [microsoft/fara](https://github.com/microsoft/fara) — ⭐ 5,129 · `Python` · AI Agent — Fara-7B is an efficient, agentic AI model developed by Microsoft for autonomous computer use, capable of performing on-device tasks such as clicking, scrolling, and filling forms without user intervention.
- [Robbyant/lingbot-world](https://github.com/Robbyant/lingbot-world) — ⭐ 3,714 · `Python` · Framework — An open-source project focused on advancing interactive video world models with long-term memory capabilities. It aims to simulate video worlds and generate content from images to video.
- [danijar/dreamerv3](https://github.com/danijar/dreamerv3) — ⭐ 3,297 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains DreamerV3, a reinforcement learning agent that masters diverse domains using learned world models. It focuses on cross-domain reinforcement learning with these models.
- [Robbyant/lingbot-world-v2](https://github.com/Robbyant/lingbot-world-v2) — ⭐ 717 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository explores "LingBot-World-Infinity," an AI world model designed to create infinite, coherent virtual worlds with versatile interactions, specifically tackling the issue of "autoregressive drift" in such models.
- [AIScientists-Dev/WorldSeed](https://github.com/AIScientists-Dev/WorldSeed) — ⭐ 684 · `Python` · Framework — WorldSeed is a multi-agent world engine designed for AI agents to live, interact, compete, and form alliances within a simulated environment. It allows users to define rules, goals, and secrets for the agents, then observe complex emergent behaviors.
- [galilai-group/stable-worldmodel](https://github.com/galilai-group/stable-worldmodel) — ⭐ 669 · `Python` · Framework — A platform designed for conducting and evaluating research on world models, particularly focusing on their reproducibility. It provides an evaluation suite that allows for testing models under various environmental shifts like color, lighting, and size.
- [facebookresearch/eb\_jepa](https://github.com/facebookresearch/eb_jepa) — ⭐ 591 · `Python` · Library — This open-source library provides examples of Joint Embedding Predictive Architectures (JEPAs) for learning representations from images, video, and action-conditioned video, supporting self-supervised learning for prediction and planning.
- [amap-cvlab/ABot-World](https://github.com/amap-cvlab/ABot-World) — ⭐ 464 · `Python` · Application — ABot-World is a project focused on the rollout of an infinite, interactive world simulation designed to run efficiently on a single desktop GPU. It functions as an application for creating and interacting with dynamic virtual environments, leveraging 'world model' concepts.
- [shengshu-ai/minWM](https://github.com/shengshu-ai/minWM) — ⭐ 338 · `Python` · Framework — This project offers a minimal and elegant framework, accompanied by a tutorial, for developing and experimenting with real-time interactive world models. It provides the codebase and resources for research into and implementation of such models.
- [AlayaLab/AlayaWorld](https://github.com/AlayaLab/AlayaWorld) — ⭐ 305 · Application — AlayaWorld is an open-source, full-stack interactive long-horizon world model capable of generating explorable, playable 720p virtual worlds. It creates these worlds in real-time from multimodal inputs such as text, images, or video clips.
- [facebookresearch/jepa-wms](https://github.com/facebookresearch/jepa-wms) — ⭐ 240 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides code, data, and pretrained weights for the research paper on Joint-Embedding Predictive World Models (JEPA-WMS), designed for implementing and studying models focused on physical planning tasks.
- [worldbench/WorldLens](https://github.com/worldbench/WorldLens) — ⭐ 220 · `Python` · Paper Code — WorldLens is a research project and framework for the full-spectrum evaluation of driving world models in real-world scenarios, presented at CVPR 2026. It focuses on assessing generative AI for autonomous driving through comprehensive benchmarks.
- [OpenEnvision/WorldFoundry](https://github.com/OpenEnvision/WorldFoundry) — ⭐ 161 · `Python` · Framework — WorldFoundry offers a unified open-source infrastructure for world model research, covering inference, evaluation, and asset management. It supports various applications including video generation and 3D/4D representations for embodied and physical AI.
- [datawhalechina/learn-world-model](https://github.com/datawhalechina/learn-world-model) — ⭐ 139 · `TypeScript` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides an open-source course by Datawhale, designed to teach the principles, architecture, and implementation of world models. It includes five lectures and projects covering topics from VAE and Dreamer to evaluation dashboards, emphasizing hands-on learning.
- [nv-tlabs/Gamma-World](https://github.com/nv-tlabs/Gamma-World) — ⭐ 132 · Paper Code — This repository contains the implementation of "Gamma-World", a generative multi-agent world model designed to simulate complex environments beyond two-player interactions. It's used for research in multi-agent systems and world modeling.
- [AbdelStark/worldforge](https://github.com/AbdelStark/worldforge) — ⭐ 124 · `Python` · Agent Framework — Worldforge is a framework for building and testing AI workflows based on world models, specifically designed for physical AI systems. It provides tools for composing, tracing, evaluating, and benchmarking these workflows.
- [AMAP-ML/DreamX-World](https://github.com/AMAP-ML/DreamX-World) — ⭐ 120 · `Python` · Model Weights — DreamX-World is a general-purpose interactive world model trained on diverse data, including Unreal Engine environments, gameplay footage, and real-world videos. It leverages reinforcement learning and distillation for efficient inference and action following within simulated or real environments.
- [experientiallabs/world-model-harness](https://github.com/experientiallabs/world-model-harness) — ⭐ 74 · `Python` · Framework — This repository provides a 'harness' for simulating AI agent environments using world models. It's designed to facilitate research and development in AI agent behavior within complex simulated worlds.
- [H-EmbodVis/HERMESV2](https://github.com/H-EmbodVis/HERMESV2) — ⭐ 40 · `Python` · Model Weights — HERMES++ introduces a unified world model focused on comprehensively understanding and generating 3D scenes within a driving context. It aims to create a holistic system for representing and simulating complex real-world driving environments.
- [sii-research/tau-0-wm](https://github.com/sii-research/tau-0-wm) — ⭐ 39 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code and model for Tau-0-WM, a research project focused on building a world model. It is associated with a research paper and model weights are available on Hugging Face.
- [FofGofx/WorldCache](https://github.com/FofGofx/WorldCache) — ⭐ 30 · `Python` · Paper Code — WorldCache is a caching framework that accelerates AI world models, particularly diffusion world models, by utilizing heterogeneous token caching. It improves inference speed by up to 3.7x without sacrificing model quality, based on research from ICML26.
- [FrankYang-17/RealUnify](https://github.com/FrankYang-17/RealUnify) — ⭐ 27 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides RealUnify, a framework and toolkit for the evaluation and benchmarking of world models. It helps researchers assess the performance and capabilities of various world model architectures.
- [Yuntian-Gao/Fast-LeWorldModel](https://github.com/Yuntian-Gao/Fast-LeWorldModel) — ⭐ 26 · Paper Code — This repository introduces Fast-LeWM, a fast latent visual world model capable of predicting future states in parallel, aiming for faster and more accurate planning. It's a research project exploring efficient world model architectures.
- [arnavkj1995/WEAVER](https://github.com/arnavkj1995/WEAVER) — ⭐ 16 · `Python` · Paper Code — WEAVER is a research project implementing an efficient, consistent, and high-fidelity world model designed to enable advanced robot policy evaluation, improvement, and faster planning during testing.
- [yczhou001/PF-OPSD](https://github.com/yczhou001/PF-OPSD) — ⭐ 14 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository is associated with a research paper that explores how AI can integrate physical intuition, derived from world models and visual simulation, with abstract language reasoning from LLMs, studying their complementary intelligence.

</details>

## Document Understanding

Turn scanned PDFs, tables, and messy invoices into structured data agents can actually use

<details>
<summary>Show 38 repos</summary>

- [PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) — ⭐ 78,826 · `Python` · Library — PaddleOCR is a powerful, lightweight toolkit for Optical Character Recognition (OCR) that converts text from PDF or image documents into structured data. It's designed to bridge the gap between visual documents and Large Language Models, supporting over 100 languages.
- [opendatalab/MinerU](https://github.com/opendatalab/MinerU) — ⭐ 63,158 · `Python` · Library — This tool transforms complex documents like PDFs and Office files into LLM-ready markdown or JSON formats, facilitating their use in agentic workflows and RAG systems.
- [opendataloader-project/opendataloader-pdf](https://github.com/opendataloader-project/opendataloader-pdf) — ⭐ 21,871 · `Java` · Library — This open-source project is a Java-based PDF parser that extracts and structures data from PDF documents, making them accessible and ready for AI applications like Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). It focuses on automating PDF accessibility and preparing data by understanding document structure including tables and bounding boxes.
- [datalab-to/surya](https://github.com/datalab-to/surya) — ⭐ 19,776 · `Python` · Library — Surya is an OCR and document analysis tool that performs layout analysis, extracts reading order, and recognizes tables and text in over 90 languages from documents like PDFs.
- [allenai/olmocr](https://github.com/allenai/olmocr) — ⭐ 19,105 · `Python` · Library — This Python toolkit is designed to process PDFs by linearizing their content into a natural reading order and converting complex layouts, including tables and equations, into usable text. Its primary purpose is to prepare structured data from PDFs for LLM datasets and training.
- [getomni-ai/zerox](https://github.com/getomni-ai/zerox) — ⭐ 12,247 · `TypeScript` · Library — This repository offers tools for Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and document extraction, utilizing vision models to process and extract information from various document types, including PDFs. It's designed to facilitate document understanding within AI and ML systems.
- [bytedance/Dolphin](https://github.com/bytedance/Dolphin) — ⭐ 8,992 · `Python` · Paper Code — Dolphin is a multimodal model from ByteDance for document image parsing, specifically designed to accurately extract and structure complex elements like text, tables, and formulas from scanned documents and PDFs.
- [rednote-hilab/dots.ocr](https://github.com/rednote-hilab/dots.ocr) — ⭐ 8,606 · `Python` · Model Weights — dots-ocr is a 1.7B parameter open-source Vision-Language Model for multilingual document layout parsing, capable of processing text, tables, formulas, images, and PDFs in over 100 languages within a single unified system.
- [adithya-s-k/omniparse](https://github.com/adithya-s-k/omniparse) — ⭐ 6,821 · `Python` · Library — Omniparse is a Python library designed to ingest, parse, and optimize various data formats, from documents to multimedia, into structured JSON. Its primary purpose is to enhance data compatibility with GenAI frameworks and large language models (LLMs).
- [oomol-lab/pdf-craft](https://github.com/oomol-lab/pdf-craft) — ⭐ 5,504 · `Python` · Application — PDF Craft is an open-source tool designed to process PDF files, particularly scanned books, converting them into structured formats like Markdown or EPUB. It utilizes OCR and AI to extract text, clean documents by removing noise, and rebuild the book structure for improved readability.
- [mixedbread-ai/mgrep](https://github.com/mixedbread-ai/mgrep) — ⭐ 4,202 · `TypeScript` · CLI Tool — mgrep is a CLI tool for semantic search, allowing users to find similar content across various data types, including code, images, and PDFs, in a user-friendly command-line interface.
- [zcaceres/markdownify-mcp](https://github.com/zcaceres/markdownify-mcp) — ⭐ 2,800 · `TypeScript` · MCP Server — This Model Context Protocol (MCP) server converts various inputs like PDFs, images, audio, and web pages into Markdown, including OCR and speech-to-text capabilities. It is designed for easy integration into LLM toolchains.
- [chatdoc-com/OCRFlux](https://github.com/chatdoc-com/OCRFlux) — ⭐ 2,503 · `Python` · Library — OCRFlux is a lightweight yet powerful multimodal toolkit designed for advanced PDF-to-Markdown conversion, featuring robust handling of complex layouts, intricate table parsing, and seamless cross-page content merging.
- [X-PLUG/mPLUG-DocOwl](https://github.com/X-PLUG/mPLUG-DocOwl) — ⭐ 2,376 · `Python` · Model Weights — A modularized multimodal large language model designed for comprehensive document understanding, specializing in OCR-free analysis of documents, charts, and tables.
- [NanoNets/docext](https://github.com/NanoNets/docext) — ⭐ 1,972 · `Python` · Library — An on-premises toolkit for OCR-free unstructured data extraction from documents (images, PDFs), converting them into Markdown. It also includes benchmarking capabilities for document information extraction.
- [shcherbak-ai/contextgem](https://github.com/shcherbak-ai/contextgem) — ⭐ 1,829 · `Python` · Library — This tool facilitates the extraction of structured data from various document types using LLMs. It is designed for tasks like contract analysis and document intelligence, allowing effortless extraction from unstructured text.
- [MarkPDFdown/markpdfdown](https://github.com/MarkPDFdown/markpdfdown) — ⭐ 1,716 · `Python` · CLI Tool — MarkPDFdown is a high-quality tool designed to convert PDF documents into Markdown format. It leverages large language models with visual recognition for accurate transcription, making it useful for document processing.
- [Tencent-Hunyuan/HunyuanOCR](https://github.com/Tencent-Hunyuan/HunyuanOCR) — ⭐ 1,690 · `Python` · Model Weights — HunyuanOCR is an Optical Character Recognition (OCR) project, likely providing models and tools to extract text from images. It includes model weights, as indicated in the tweet context.

It performs tasks like OCR and layout analysis, with recent enhancements in handwritten OCR accuracy.
- [alibaba/Logics-Parsing](https://github.com/alibaba/Logics-Parsing) — ⭐ 1,345 · `Python` · Library — Logics-Parsing is an open-source, end-to-end document parsing model that converts complex documents, including scientific papers, reports, and handwritten notes, into structured HTML, accurately recognizing formulas, tables, and figures while filtering out noise.
- [Topdu/OpenOCR](https://github.com/Topdu/OpenOCR) — ⭐ 1,329 · `Python` · Framework — OpenOCR is an open-source toolkit for General Optical Character Recognition (OCR) research and applications, offering a unified benchmark, commercial-grade systems, and academic implementations. It is designed to extract text from images and documents.
- [huridocs/pdf-document-layout-analysis](https://github.com/huridocs/pdf-document-layout-analysis) — ⭐ 1,120 · `Python` · Self-hosted Service — This is a Docker-powered service designed for PDF document layout analysis. It segments and classifies different parts of PDF pages, identifying elements such as text, titles, pictures, and tables.
- [landing-ai/ade-python](https://github.com/landing-ai/ade-python) — ⭐ 960 · `Python` · Library — A Python library for Agentic Document Extraction (ADE), designed to classify and extract structured information from various document types, often integrating with a classification API.
- [chrisryugj/kordoc](https://github.com/chrisryugj/kordoc) — ⭐ 933 · `TypeScript` · MCP Server — This versatile tool functions as both a CLI and an MCP server, parsing various document formats including HWP, HWPX, PDF, XLSX, and DOCX, and converting them into Markdown. It streamlines document processing for integration with AI systems.
- [th1nhhdk/local\_ai\_ocr](https://github.com/th1nhhdk/local_ai_ocr) — ⭐ 767 · `Python` · Application — This is a local, offline, and portable OCR (Optical Character Recognition) software capable of processing images and PDF files. It utilizes the DeepSeek-OCR-2 AI model, which runs directly on the user's machine, to extract text.
- [SylphxAI/pdf-reader-mcp](https://github.com/SylphxAI/pdf-reader-mcp) — ⭐ 713 · `TypeScript` · MCP Server — A production-ready MCP server for efficient PDF processing, offering 5-10x faster performance through parallel processing. It features high test coverage and is designed to integrate as a component for AI agents and LLM tools.
- [brianxiadong/auto-paper-digest](https://github.com/brianxiadong/auto-paper-digest) — ⭐ 553 · `Python` · CLI Tool — An automated Python pipeline that tracks new AI papers, downloads PDFs, imports them into NotebookLM, generates video summaries, and archives them into a searchable weekly digest, helping researchers stay updated.
- [Pluviobyte/rnskill](https://github.com/Pluviobyte/rnskill) — ⭐ 531 · `Python` · Skill Pack — This repository provides a skill pack that allows AI agents, specifically mentioning Claude Code and Codex, to access and parse content including text and images from WeChat official account articles, which they cannot directly access otherwise. It bridges the gap for agents to consume content from these specific web sources.
- [run-llama/ParseBench](https://github.com/run-llama/ParseBench) — ⭐ 475 · `Python` · Paper Code — ParseBench is a benchmark designed to evaluate the document parsing capabilities of AI agents, specifically focusing on complex tasks like extracting structured information from diverse document types, including tables in PDFs. It aims to measure and improve how well agents understand and act upon real-world enterprise documents.
- [ningzimu/image-to-editable-ppt-skill](https://github.com/ningzimu/image-to-editable-ppt-skill) — ⭐ 304 · `Python` · Skill Pack — This repository provides a Codex skill that converts slide images, PDFs, and image-based PowerPoint files into fully editable PowerPoint decks. It utilizes a multi-agent system for page-by-page reconstruction, extracting text, shapes, and images to generate high-quality, editable presentations.
- [chatclimate-ai/ParseStudio](https://github.com/chatclimate-ai/ParseStudio) — ⭐ 253 · `Python` · Library — A Python package that unifies various PDF parsing engines, including Docling, PyMuPDF, and Llama Parse, to extract text, structured tables, and images from PDFs with a consistent API. It simplifies complex parsing tasks for downstream applications, often for LLMs.
- [bhimrazy/receipt-ocr](https://github.com/bhimrazy/receipt-ocr) — ⭐ 243 · `Python` · Library — An efficient OCR engine for processing receipt images and extracting structured data using Large Language Models.
- [harumiWeb/exstruct](https://github.com/harumiWeb/exstruct) — ⭐ 157 · `Python` · MCP Server — This tool converts Excel files into structured JSON, extracting tables, shapes, and charts. It's designed to facilitate LLM/RAG pipelines and enable AI agents to autonomously read and write Excel data via CLI and MCP integration.
- [Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCRv2](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCRv2) — ⭐ 100 · `Python` · Paper Code — MonkeyOCRv2 is a visual-text foundation model specifically designed for Document AI. It introduces a document-native vision encoder pretrained via joint text generation and pixel-level reconstruction.
- [KyrieCheungYep/ky-markdown-rebuilder](https://github.com/KyrieCheungYep/ky-markdown-rebuilder) — ⭐ 99 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This Python-based skill or tool is designed to convert visually complex documents, such as PDFs, PPTs, scanned images, and long screenshots, into page-aligned Markdown format. It leverages visual models and OCR to reconstruct document structure and content, including tables, while preserving links to original pages.
- [StarTrail-org/PixelRAG](https://github.com/StarTrail-org/PixelRAG) — ⭐ 47 · `Python` · Application — PixelRAG is a system for web information retrieval and search that operates on pixel-native data (screenshots of web pages) rather than traditional HTML parsing. It uses Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to 'read' and process content from millions of web pages.
- [phuc-nt/scan-to-ebook](https://github.com/phuc-nt/scan-to-ebook) — ⭐ 41 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This command-line tool converts scanned photos or PDFs of physical books into clean EPUB electronic books. It leverages a visual model via OpenRouter for OCR, intelligently extracting text and identifying context like titles and proper nouns to enhance accuracy, creating readable ebooks for platforms like Apple Books or Kindle.
- [run-llama/liteparse-server](https://github.com/run-llama/liteparse-server) — ⭐ 11 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — This is an Express.js HTTP server built on top of LiteParse, designed to provide a lightweight backend for document parsing. It can process PDFs, images, and Office documents, and generate page screenshots, acting as a self-hosted infrastructure for document understanding.

</details>

## Multimodal Generation

Type a prompt, get images, audio, and video back from a single unified model

<summary>Show 66 repos</summary>

- [danny-avila/LibreChat](https://github.com/danny-avila/LibreChat) — ⭐ 37,353 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — LibreChat is an open-source, self-hostable, enhanced ChatGPT clone that aggregates various mainstream AI models and APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, etc.). - [smthemex/ComfyUI\_SenseNova\_U1](https://github.com/smthemex/ComfyUI_SenseNova_U1) — ⭐ 32 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This repository provides a custom node for ComfyUI that integrates the SenseNova-U1 model, designed for unifying multimodal understanding and generation tasks using the NEO-Unify architecture. It supports GGUF models for diverse multimodal applications.
- [JpAndreBTA/Nexus-BTA](https://github.com/JpAndreBTA/Nexus-BTA) — ⭐ 25 · `HTML` · Application — Nexus BTA is a local AI studio for ComfyUI, providing a clean interface for image, video, 3D, and workflow experimentation. It supports various models like SD 1.5, SDXL, Flux, and ControlNet, simplifying common controls for generative AI tasks.
- [banodoco/hivemind](https://github.com/banodoco/hivemind) — ⭐ 17 · `Shell` · Skill Pack — This project offers a drop-in skill to enhance coding agents like Claude Code or Codex. It enables them to search Banodoco Discord feeds for best practices in video and image generation, providing agents with access to multimodal content creation knowledge.

</details>

## Other

Cross-modal odds and ends: audio-visual fusion, embodied perception, and experiments that don't fit a box yet

<details>
<summary>Show 21 repos</summary>

- [google-gemini/gemini-cli](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli) — ⭐ 103,230 · `TypeScript` · AI Agent — This is an open-source AI agent that integrates the capabilities of Gemini directly into the command-line interface.
- [microsoft/OmniParser](https://github.com/microsoft/OmniParser) — ⭐ 24,728 · `Jupyter Notebook` · CLI Tool — A screen parsing tool that extracts structured elements from UI screenshots. It facilitates the development of pure vision-based GUI agents capable of understanding and interacting with graphical user interfaces.
- [titanwings/colleague-skill](https://github.com/titanwings/colleague-skill) — ⭐ 18,756 · `Python` · Paper Code — A project exploring the concept of 'Digital Life 1.0' by transforming memories and farewells into 'warm skills'. It provides code related to a research paper and features a community gallery for these digital capabilities.
- [google-gemini/cookbook](https://github.com/google-gemini/cookbook) — ⭐ 17,165 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository serves as a cookbook with examples and guides, primarily in Jupyter Notebooks, for effectively utilizing the Google Gemini API. It helps users integrate and explore the capabilities of the Gemini multimodal AI model.
- [pipecat-ai/pipecat](https://github.com/pipecat-ai/pipecat) — ⭐ 12,194 · `Python` · Agent Framework — An open-source Python framework designed for building real-time voice and multimodal conversational AI agents.
- [livekit/agents](https://github.com/livekit/agents) — ⭐ 10,593 · `Python` · Agent Framework — Livekit Agents is a Python framework for building real-time AI agents that handle voice and video interactions. It enables the creation of conversational AI applications with capabilities like telephony integration.
- [TencentQQGYLab/AppAgent](https://github.com/TencentQQGYLab/AppAgent) — ⭐ 6,727 · `Python` · Agent Framework — AppAgent is an LLM-based multimodal agent framework that enables AI agents to operate smartphone applications by mimicking human taps and swipes.
- [teamchong/pxpipe](https://github.com/teamchong/pxpipe) — ⭐ 6,146 · `TypeScript` · Library — Pxpipe is a utility designed to optimize LLM token usage, specifically for models like Fable 5, by rendering text context as images. This technique allows for potentially more efficient multimodal input to large language models.
- [aiming-lab/SimpleMem](https://github.com/aiming-lab/SimpleMem) — ⭐ 3,217 · `Python` · Library — SimpleMem provides an efficient lifelong memory system for multimodal LLM agents, enabling them to store and retrieve long-term memories from both text and various multimodal data sources. It utilizes techniques such as RAG, semantic search, and knowledge graphs to enhance agent capabilities.
- [snapotter-hq/SnapOtter](https://github.com/snapotter-hq/SnapOtter) — ⭐ 2,027 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — SnapOtter is an open-source, self-hosted file-processing tool that handles images, videos, audio, PDFs, and other documents. It offers features like conversion, compression, OCR, transcription, and local AI processing via a UI, REST API, and pipelines, ensuring data privacy by keeping all files on-premise.
- [microsoft/Magma](https://github.com/microsoft/Magma) — ⭐ 1,926 · `Python` · Model Weights — Magma is a foundation model designed for multimodal AI agents, presented at CVPR 2025.
- [OpenSenseNova/SenseNova-U1](https://github.com/OpenSenseNova/SenseNova-U1) — ⭐ 1,131 · `Python` · Model Weights — SenseNova-U1 is a native unified multimodal AI model, built from first principles with a comprehensive paradigm. It is recognized as a trending model on Hugging Face.
- [facebookresearch/tuna-2](https://github.com/facebookresearch/tuna-2) — ⭐ 612 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the official implementation of Tuna-2, a research project demonstrating that pixel embeddings can outperform vision encoders for unified understanding and generation tasks. It explores advanced techniques for multimodal vision models.
- [sonichi/sutando](https://github.com/sonichi/sutando) — ⭐ 154 · `Python` · Agent Framework — A framework for creating personal AI agents that learn and improve autonomously using voice and vision, capable of tasks like code reviews and presentations.
- [hanxiao/omni-macos](https://github.com/hanxiao/omni-macos) — ⭐ 123 · `Swift` · Application — An application for macOS that enables on-device semantic search across local files including text, code, images, audio, and video. It unifies various data modalities into a single vector space for efficient retrieval on Apple Silicon Macs.
- [modelstudioai/cli](https://github.com/modelstudioai/cli) — ⭐ 118 · `TypeScript` · CLI Tool — An official command-line interface (CLI) for Alibaba Cloud's AI platform, Model Studio. It enables AI Agent frameworks to access a wide range of AI capabilities, including text dialogue (Qwen), multimodal understanding (images, audio, video), image/video generation and editing, speech synthesis and recognition, knowledge base retrieval, and web search through structured tool calls.
- [MajoorWaldi/ComfyUI-Majoor-AssetsManager](https://github.com/MajoorWaldi/ComfyUI-Majoor-AssetsManager) — ⭐ 103 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — Majoor Assets Manager is an advanced asset browser for ComfyUI. It helps users manage, organize, and view generated assets like images, videos, audio, and 3D outputs directly within ComfyUI, offering features such as search, metadata extraction, rating, and tagging.
- [kesai-labs/py123d](https://github.com/kesai-labs/py123d) — ⭐ 66 · `Python` · Library — This repository provides 123D, a unified Python library designed for handling multi-modal data in autonomous driving applications.
- [zfkarl/Orchestra-o1](https://github.com/zfkarl/Orchestra-o1) — ⭐ 43 · `Python` · Agent Framework — This is the official repository for Orchestra-o1, a system focused on omnimodal agent orchestration, likely involving coordinating AI agents across various modalities.
- [MinghoKwok/MemEye](https://github.com/MinghoKwok/MemEye) — ⭐ 25 · `Python` · Framework — MemEye is a visual-centric evaluation framework designed to assess the memory capabilities of multimodal AI agents. It provides tools and benchmarks to evaluate how well these agents retain and recall information from visual and other modalities.
- [zjunlp/LightMem-Ego](https://github.com/zjunlp/LightMem-Ego) — ⭐ 22 · `Python` · AI Agent — LightMem-Ego is an AI memory system designed to act as a personal assistant, capturing and processing multimodal information from everyday life. It serves as a codebase for research into multimodal AI agents with memory capabilities.
